name: MCP Manager Control

meta.description: Manage json-mcp-manager via MCP — list, start, stop, restart instances, view logs, upload tools, edit code and more. Security levels are enforced server-side.

manifest.title: MCP Manager Control
manifest.author: Torsten Feustel
manifest.description: Full management of json-mcp-manager via MCP. Respects server-side security levels.

specs.name: delete_instance
specs.description: Delete an MCP server instance and its config. Requires upload or full edit mode.

specs.name: export_tool
specs.description: Export an MCP server tool as OpenWebUI-compatible JSON. Requires full edit mode.

instance_id.description: The instance ID (used as tool id)

name.description: Display name for the exported tool

specs.name: get_install_log
specs.description: Get the installation log for an MCP server instance.

specs.name: get_instance
specs.description: Get status and details of a specific MCP server instance.

specs.name: get_instance_config
specs.description: Get the full configuration of an MCP server instance including values.

specs.name: get_runtime_log
specs.description: Get the runtime log for an MCP server instance.

specs.name: get_settings
specs.description: Get current manager settings: auth status, edit mode, host, port and MCP token status.

specs.name: get_tool_code
specs.description: Get the Python source code of an MCP server tool. Requires full edit mode.

specs.name: get_tool_template
specs.description:
Returns the Python code template AND the complete JSON upload format.

Always call this before writing or uploading a new tool.
The response contains two sections:
1. The Python code template (for the 'content' field in the upload JSON)
2. The exact JSON array format that upload_tool() expects

specs.name: list_instances
specs.description: List all MCP server instances with their status, port and URL.

specs.name: reinstall_instance
specs.description: Reinstall dependencies for an MCP server instance.

specs.name: restart_instance
specs.description: Restart an MCP server instance.

specs.name: restart_manager
specs.description: Restart the json-mcp-manager process. The manager will come back up automatically.

specs.name: save_tool_code
specs.description:
Replace the Python code of an EXISTING instance — this is the 'edit code' action.

Use this (NOT upload_tool) whenever an instance with this ID already exists,
e.g. to fix a bug, add a comment or add a method. The manager validates the
code before saving and rejects invalid code with an error list. On success
the instance restarts automatically, so the change is live immediately.
Requires full edit mode.

instance_id.description: The instance ID whose code to replace

code.description: The complete new Python source code (the full file, not a diff)

specs.name: start_instance
specs.description: Start an MCP server instance.

specs.name: stop_instance
specs.description: Stop a running MCP server instance.

specs.name: upload_tool
specs.description:
Create and install a NEW tool instance from a JSON definition.

Only for NEW tools: fails if the ID already exists — in that case use
save_tool_code(instance_id, code) to change the existing tool instead.
The manager validates the Python code on upload and rejects invalid code
with an error list. Requires upload or full edit mode.

Workflow for a new tool:
1. get_tool_template() — get the exact JSON format and code template
2. validate_tool_code(code) — repeat until there are no errors
3. upload_tool(json_content) — json_content must be a JSON ARRAY (not an object)
4. start_instance(id) — uploaded instances do not start automatically

json_content.description: JSON array string — call get_tool_template() to see the format

specs.name: validate_tool_code
specs.description:
Validate Python tool code: syntax check, runtime inspection and schema generation.

ALWAYS call this before upload_tool or save_tool_code and fix every reported
error first — the manager rejects invalid code anyway, so validating early
saves a round trip. Returns valid, errors, warnings, the generated tool
specs and the Valves defaults. Requires full edit mode.

code.description: The Python source code to validate

specs.name: create_tool
specs.description: Create and install a NEW instance from raw Python code in ONE step (preferred over upload_tool — no JSON wrapping). Installs dependencies, validates in the instance venv, fills valve defaults and saves config. Does not auto-start. Requires upload or full edit mode.

code.description: The complete Python source code (the full file)

id.description: Unique snake_case identifier (becomes the MCP server ID)

description.description: Short description shown in UI and to the AI

requirements.description: Optional comma-separated pip packages (overrides the docstring requirements line)

port.description: Optional fixed port (1024-65535); omit to auto-assign. Fails with 409 if taken.

specs.name: update_instance_values
specs.description: Set/merge Valve values (configuration) of an existing instance, e.g. an API key or base URL. Only send the keys to change; others are kept. Does NOT restart — values take effect after a restart, so ask the user whether to restart afterwards. Requires upload or full edit mode.

values.description: Mapping of valve name to value

specs.name: update_instance_dependencies
specs.description: Replace the pip dependencies of an existing instance and reinstall them into its venv. Requires upload or full edit mode.

dependencies.description: Full list of pip package specs (replaces the old list)

specs.name: update_instance_venv
specs.description: Move an instance to a different virtual environment. Sets the venv, reinstalls deps there and restarts if running. Creates the venv on demand. Requires upload or full edit mode.
